metadata.title: Transparency and Targeting of Political Advertisement Vocabulary

description.en: Something that happens at a particular place and time, organised by one or more agents for a particular purpose, and is of interest to a general audience. This definition excludes natural events such as earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.

rawTags.definition-en: Something that happens at a particular place and time, organised by one or more agents for a particular purpose, and is of interest to a general audience. This definition excludes natural events such as earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.

description.en: The website of the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the URL of the official website of the public event. For elections or referendums linked to a political advertisement, this should link to official information about the modalities for participation, as required by Article 12(1)(h) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 10 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

rawTags.definition-en: The website of the Public Event.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the URL of the official website of the public event. For elections or referendums linked to a political advertisement, this should link to official information about the modalities for participation, as required by Article 12(1)(h) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 10 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: The association of a temporal entity (instant or interval) to any thing.

usage.en: Provide the temporal instant or interval associated with the public event. For elections and referendums, this should correspond to the date(s) of the event as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the temporal instant or interval associated with the public event. For elections and referendums, this should correspond to the date(s) of the event as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.
rawTags.definition-en: The association of a temporal entity (instant or interval) to any thing.

description.en: The name given to the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the official name given to the public event. For elections and referendums, the title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected (e.g., 'parliamentary elections') or to a specific type (e.g., 'constitutional referendum') as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the official name given to the public event. For elections and referendums, the title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected (e.g., 'parliamentary elections') or to a specific type (e.g., 'constitutional referendum') as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.
rawTags.definition: The name given to the Public Event.
rawTags.definition-en: The name given to the Public Event.

description.en: Information describing the financial aspects of a political campaign,including sources of funding,expenditures,and financial disclosures.

usage.en: Use this class to provide aggregated financial information for a political advertising campaign, including total amount and currency, as required for transparency and reporting under EU Regulation 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: Information describing the financial aspects of a political campaign,including sources of funding,expenditures,and financial disclosures.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to provide aggregated financial information for a political advertising campaign, including total amount and currency, as required for transparency and reporting under EU Regulation 2024/900.

description.en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency. Annex II, 3.6

rawTags.definition-en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.
rawTags.usageNote-en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency. Annex II, 3.6

description.en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

rawTags.definition-en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.
rawTags.usageNote-en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

description.en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.

usage.en: Use this class to describe an election event that is referenced or linked to a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to an election, the title, level, and date of the election must be disclosed in the transparency notice as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The level must be indicated as EU, national, regional, or local.

rawTags.definition-en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to describe an election event that is referenced or linked to a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to an election, the title, level, and date of the election must be disclosed in the transparency notice as required by Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The level must be indicated as EU, national, regional, or local.

description.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

rawTags.definition-en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

description.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

rawTags.definition-en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

description.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the Election Event (e.g., 'parliamentary elections', 'constitutional referendum'). The title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected, as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

rawTags.definition-en: A name given to the resource.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the Election Event (e.g., 'parliamentary elections', 'constitutional referendum'). The title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected, as per Annex II, Template 3, note 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the election is conducted. Annex II, 3.9

rawTags.definition-en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the election is conducted. Annex II, 3.9

description.en: Financial support provided for a project, activity, or entity, typically by an organisation, government, or individual.

usage.en: Use this class to represent the overall financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including both direct and indirect funding sources, as required for transparency under EU Regulation 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial support provided for a project, activity, or entity, typically by an organisation, government, or individual.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to represent the overall financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including both direct and indirect funding sources, as required for transparency under EU Regulation 2024/900.

description.en: Financial information related to the political advertisement.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial information related to the political advertisement.

description.en: The nature of the funding (e.g., public, private, mixed).

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "Public", "Private", or "Public and private".

rawTags.definition-en: The nature of the funding (e.g., public, private, mixed).
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "Public", "Private", or "Public and private".

description.en: Indicates whether the origin of the funding is from the EU, from outside the EU.

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "EU", "Non-EU", or "EU and non-EU". If possible, align with official EU country groupings.

rawTags.definition-en: Indicates whether the origin of the funding is from the EU, from outside the EU.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "EU", "Non-EU", or "EU and non-EU". If possible, align with official EU country groupings.

description.en: Financial information related to the campaign.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial information related to the campaign.

description.en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.

usage.en: Describe the methodology used to calculate the aggregated amounts and value of other benefits received by the providers of political advertising services, including whether the figures reflect invoiced, budgeted, and/or charged amounts and whether VAT is included. For in-kind benefits, indicate the valuation method used. This information is mandatory in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(f) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 8 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

rawTags.definition-en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Describe the methodology used to calculate the aggregated amounts and value of other benefits received by the providers of political advertising services, including whether the figures reflect invoiced, budgeted, and/or charged amounts and whether VAT is included. For in-kind benefits, indicate the valuation method used. This information is mandatory in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(f) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 8 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: A project,program,or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.

usage.en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative that is referenced or promoted by a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to a legislative or regulatory initiative, the title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The title may be shortened.

rawTags.definition-en: A project,program,or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative that is referenced or promoted by a political advertisement. When a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link to a legislative or regulatory initiative, the title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice under Article 12(1)(g) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 9 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The title may be shortened.

description.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

description.en: An advertisement with a political subject matter,including information about its content,format,language,publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any advertisement with political content,including its metadata and links to related legislative or voting processes.

rawTags.definition-en: An advertisement with a political subject matter,including information about its content,format,language,publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to represent any advertisement with political content,including its metadata and links to related legislative or voting processes.

description.en: The end of a period or interval.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

rawTags.definition-en: The end of a period or interval.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

description.en: The beginning of a period or interval.

description.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Specify the format or medium of the political advertisement (e.g. video, audio, display, printed, online). This determines which labelling requirements apply under Annex I of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410 (television/radio, printed media, or digital medium).

rawTags.definition-en: The nature or genre of the resource.

description.en: A topic of the resource.

usage.en: Provide a topic or keyword describing the subject matter of the political advertisement. This helps contextualise the advertisement and supports identification of its political subject matter as required for the assessment under Article 8 of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide a topic or keyword describing the subject matter of the political advertisement. This helps contextualise the advertisement and supports identification of its political subject matter as required for the assessment under Article 8 of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.
rawTags.definition-en: A topic of the resource.

description.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the language in which the political advertisement is written or spoken, using a controlled vocabulary (e.g. BCP 47 language tags or ISO 639). Transparency notices must be written in the language of the political advertisement as required by Article 12(3) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

description.en: The set of criteria, characteristics, or parameters used to define the intended audience or recipients of a political advertisement.

usage.en: Use this class to document all targeting and ad-delivery techniques based on personal data used for the political advertisement. Population of this class is mandatory when online political advertisements use such techniques, under Article 12(1)(l) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 1416 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The label of the political advertisement must also include a statement about the use of targeting or ad-delivery techniques under Article 11(1)(d) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: The set of criteria, characteristics, or parameters used to define the intended audience or recipients of a political advertisement.

Population of this class is mandatory when online political advertisements use such techniques, under Article 12(1)(l) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 1416 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410. The label of the political advertisement must also include a statement about the use of targeting or ad-delivery techniques under Article 11(1)(d) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

description.en: TODO

usage.en: A Legal Entity able to transact business, typically registered with a body able to confer legal status such as a national business register. It is able to trade, is legally liable for its actions, accounts, tax affairs etc. This makes Legal Entities distinct from the concept of organisations or groups. Many organisations exist that are not Legal Entities, yet to the outside world they have staff, hierarchies, locations etc. Other organisations exist that are an umbrella for several Legal Entities (universities are often good examples of this). This vocabulary is concerned solely with registered Legal Entities and does not attempt to cover all possible trading bodies.

rawTags.definition-en: null
rawTags.usageNote-en: A Legal Entity able to transact business, typically registered with a body able to confer legal status such as a national business register. It is able to trade, is legally liable for its actions, accounts, tax affairs etc. This makes Legal Entities distinct from the concept of organisations or groups. Many organisations exist that are not Legal Entities, yet to the outside world they have staff, hierarchies, locations etc. Other organisations exist that are an umbrella for several Legal Entities (universities are often good examples of this). This vocabulary is concerned solely with registered Legal Entities and does not attempt to cover all possible trading bodies.

description.en: Information about the analytics performed on the advertisement or its audience.

usage.en: Provide information about analytical techniques used to assess or optimise the reach and impact of the advertisement, such as audience segmentation, performance metrics, or engagement analysis.

Must be populated when the political advertisement uses targeting or ad-delivery techniques based on personal data, under Article 12(1)(l) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 1416 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: The entity that provides financial or other support for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the sponsor of the political advertisement. The identity of the sponsor, including name, email, postal address (where made public), and place of establishment is mandatory under Article 12(1)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

The identity of the sponsor, including name, email, postal address (where made public), and place of establishment is mandatory under Article 12(1)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, point 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: The date after which a resource or record is no longer required to be retained.

usage.en: Indicates the final date by which the transparency notice and any modifications must be retained, in accordance with applicable legal, regulatory, or organisational requirements. For political advertising transparency notices under Regulation (EU) 2024/900, this is at least seven years after the last publication of the related political advertisement. The value should be calculated as the date of last publication plus the required retention period (e.g., 7 years).

Aggregated amounts, values of in-kind benefits, their public/private and EU/non-EU origin, and the calculation methodology are mandatory under Article 12(1)(d), (e), and (f) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and Annex II, Template 3, points 5-8 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: A self-employed person,company,or organization that has legal rights and obligations.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any legal entity involved in the context of a political advertisement, including the sponsor, the paying entity, or the legal entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. This class supports the requirements for transparency notices under EU Regulation 2024/900, which mandate disclosure of the identity, contact details, and registration information for all such entities.

description.en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.

usage.en:
Provide the name under which the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity is legally registered.
The legal name is different from the alternative (trade) name which is a pseudonym used by companies that do not operate under their registered company name.
A business might have more than one legal name, particularly in countries with more than one official language. In such cases, and where the encoding technology allows, the language of the string should be identified.

description.en: A spatial object that in a human-readable way identifies a fixed location.

usage.en: Use this class to represent a structured postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity in a transparency notice. Address information must include at minimum the street, number, city, postal code and country as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410 and Article 12(1)(a) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900.

description.en: The name of a passage or way through from one location to another.

usage.en: A thoroughfare is usually a street, but it might be a waterway or some other feature. For example, "Avenue des Champs-Élysées".

description.en: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

usage.en:
The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

The key difference between a locator designator and a locator name is that the latter is a proper name and is unlikely to include digits. For example, "Shumann, Berlaymont" is a meeting room within the European Commission headquarters for which locator name is more appropriate than locator.

usage.en: Provide the city or municipality of the address. Mandatory component of any postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity, as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: The name of the uppermost level of the address, almost always a country.

usage.en: Provide the country of the address using a controlled vocabulary. Mandatory component of any postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity, as required by Annex II, Template 3, note 1 of Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1410.

description.en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an Address.

usage.en:
The concept of adding a globally unique identifier for each instance of an address is a crucial part of the INSPIRE data spec. A number of EU countries have already implemented an ID (a UUID) in their Address Register/gazetteer, among them Denmark. OASIS xAL also includes an address identifier. It is the address Identifier that allows an address to be represented in a format other than INSPIRE whilst remaining conformant to the Core Vocabulary.

The INSPIRE method of representing addresses is very detailed, designed primarily for use in databases of addresses. Whilst data that is published in full conformance with the INSPIRE data structure can be made available using theCore Location Vocabulary the reverse is not true since the Core Vocabulary allows much greater flexibility.

Many datasets that include address data as one piece of information about something else are likely to have that data in simpler formats. These might be tailored to the specific need of the dataset, follow a national norm, or make use of a standard like vCard.

rawTags.usageNote-en:

A number of EU countries have already implemented an ID (a UUID) in their Address Register/gazetteer, among them Denmark. OASIS xAL also includes an address identifier. It is the address Identifier that allows an address to be represented in a format other than INSPIRE whilst remaining conformant to the Core Vocabulary.

The INSPIRE method of representing addresses is very detailed, designed primarily for use in databases of addresses. Whilst data that is published in full conformance with the INSPIRE data structure can be made available using theCore Location Vocabulary the reverse is not true since the Core Vocabulary allows much greater flexibility.

Many datasets that include address data as one piece of information about something else are likely to have that data in simpler formats. These might be tailored to the specific need of the dataset, follow a national norm, or make use of a standard like vCard.
rawTags.definition-en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an Address.

description.en: A individual human being who may be dead or alive,but not imaginary.

usage.en: The fact that a person in the context of Core Person Vocabulary cannot be imaginary makes person:Person a subclass of both foaf:Person and schema:Person which both cover imaginary characters as well as real people. The Person Class is a subclass of the more general 'Agent' class.

description.en: Entity that is able to carry out action.

usage.en: In compliance with the description from FOAF, an Agent is considered as any entity that is able to carry out actions. The Agent class acts as a generic element which can be further specified by implementers for their usages, for example by defining the Person class from the Core Person Vocabulary or Organization from W3C's Organization Ontology as a subclasses of Agent. This Person or Organization can then issue a certain Requirement or be concerned by an Evidence provided.

description.en: The text data type is a combination of a string and a language identifier.

usage.en:
It is useful for names and descriptions that are available in multiple languages. Where this is so, each version of the data should be included and each one associated with the relevant language identifier. RFC 3066 provides a commonly used set of identifiers for natural languages. This is the set recognised by UN/CEFACT and XML Schema.
Languages are represented by two character codes, optionally followed by a locale definition such as "de" meaning German and "de-at" meaning "German as spoken in Austria."

description.en: Date represents top-open intervals of exactly one day in length on the timelines of dateTime, beginning on the beginning moment of each day, up to but not including the beginning moment of the next day).  For non-timezoned values, the top-open intervals disjointly cover the non-timezoned timeline, one per day.  For timezoned values, the intervals begin at every minute and therefore overlap.

description.en: Integer is ·derived· from decimal by fixing the value of ·fractionDigits· to be 0 and disallowing the trailing decimal point.  This results in the standard mathematical concept of the integer numbers.  The ·value space· of integer is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0,1,2,...}.  The ·base type· of integer is decimal.
